title: Kinematics Graph Problem Generator API
description: Generate physics motion-graph (x-t / v-t / a-t) problem images programmatically. POST a problem spec to /api/generate; receive PNG/DOCX/TXT/ZIP file paths (or inline dataURLs).

endpoints.POST /api/generate: Generate a problem (images + docx + txt + bundle zip) for one problem spec
endpoints.GET /api/output/:session/:file: Serve a generated file by session+filename

graph.description: A graph spec is either a MotionGraph spec (kind='vt'|'xt') or a StepMotionGraph spec (kind='vt-step'). The 'kind' field discriminates between the two shapes. Used for 'source' and for 'choices.distractors' entries.

motionGraph.description: Matches MotionGraph.toJSON()/fromJSON() (js/motion.js). Continuous piecewise-linear graph (vertices joined by straight lines).
motionGraph.kind: 'vt' (velocity-time, primary input) | 'xt' (position-time, line segments only — no parabolic arcs by hand)
motionGraph.points: Array of {t:int, value:multiple-of-0.5}, at least 2. Order doesn't matter (sorted internally).
motionGraph.x0: Number (default 0). Initial position used as the integration constant when kind='vt' (x-t is derived by integrating v-t from x0).
motionGraph.label: Optional label string (e.g. 'A').

stepMotionGraph.description: Matches StepMotionGraph.toJSON()/fromJSON() (js/step-motion.js). Staircase (Gauss-symbol / floor-function) v-t graph: piecewise-constant velocity over unit-width intervals [tStart+i, tStart+i+1), with intentional discontinuous jumps at interval boundaries (e.g. approximating free fall as 'velocity increases by one step every second').
stepMotionGraph.kind: 'vt-step' (fixed — always velocity-time; cannot be used as an x-t source)
stepMotionGraph.tStart: Integer. Start time of the first interval [tStart, tStart+1).
stepMotionGraph.values: Array of numbers, at least 1 element. values[i] = constant velocity over interval [tStart+i, tStart+i+1). Each value is snapped to a multiple of 0.5 by the model — the API does not enforce this on input.
stepMotionGraph.x0: Number (default 0). Initial position used as the integration constant for x-t derivation (same role as MotionGraph.x0).
stepMotionGraph.label: Optional label string (e.g. 'A').

grid.autoYRange: If yMin/yMax are not specified, the y-range is auto-adjusted to fit the source graph and all derived curves (xt/vt/at), symmetric about 0.

cellSize.description: Optional fixed cell pixel size. null = auto (580x200 logical px). Range: 15..120.

style: 'bw' (default, black-and-white print friendly) | 'color' (browser/color print, blue=x-t green=v-t red=a-t) | custom STYLE_PRESETS-shaped object

fontSize.description: Optional integer 8..24 (default 12). Font size for all in-graph text (axis labels, tick numbers, origin 'O', legend, '?' markers). When > 12, canvas padding is scaled by fontSize/12 automatically (plot area size is preserved; only the margins grow). Same behavior as the browser UI's 文字サイズ設定 slider.

displayPreset.description: Optional preset name applying a whole set of display toggles at once (same as the browser UI's 表示項目設定 preset buttons). Applied BEFORE 'display', whose per-key values override the preset. Defined in js/styles.js DISPLAY_PRESETS.

values.all: Everything visible (default behavior when neither displayPreset nor display is given).
values.qualitative: Hide grid, tick numbers, units and legend; keep axes, axis labels, y=0 baseline and '?' markers. For shape-discussion problems that still name the quantities.
values.qualitative-grid: Same as 'qualitative' but with the grid visible (graph-paper style).
values.shape-only: Hide everything that identifies the physical quantity (axis labels, units, tick numbers, grid, legend); keep only axis arrows, y=0 baseline, the curve and '?' markers. Designed for graphChoice problems where labels would give away which graph kind is shown.

display.description: Optional per-key display toggles, overriding the displayPreset (or the all-visible default). All keys boolean. Applied uniformly to every canvas of the problem (source, blank answer grids, model answers and choices).

keys.showTicksX: Tick marks and numbers on the t axis.
keys.showTicksY: Tick marks and numbers on the value axis.
keys.showUnitX: The unit part '[s]' of the t-axis label (label text itself stays).
keys.showUnitY: The unit part '[m]'/'[m/s]'/'[m/s²]' of the value-axis label.
keys.showAxisLabelX: The whole t-axis label ('時刻 t [s]').
keys.showAxisLabelY: The whole value-axis label ('速度 v [m/s]' etc.). Turn off together with showAxisLabelX for shape-only choices.
keys.showZeroLine: Dashed y=0 baseline drawn when showAxes=false (when axes are visible the t axis itself is the y=0 line, so this key only matters with showAxes=false).
keys.showUndefinedMark: COMBINED toggle for the undefined-instant '?' markers AND the area fill (displacement shading). Kept ON in every preset by design (曖昧さを非表示にしない); can only be turned off here explicitly.

graphConversion.name: Graph conversion — derive other motion graphs from a hand-drawn one
graphConversion.description: Given a hand-drawn v-t or x-t graph, ask the student to draw the corresponding graph(s) of other kinds (e.g. 'given v-t, draw x-t and a-t').
graphConversion.askFor: 'xt' | 'vt' | 'at', or an array of these (multiple targets in one problem). Must not include sourceKind.
graphConversion.output: question.canvases = [source graph, blank answer grid per askFor item]; answer.canvases = [derived curve per askFor item]

numeric.name: Numeric / descriptive (free-response only — never multiple choice)
numeric.description: Generates a free-response numeric or descriptive question derived from the given graph. Per CLAUDE.md design rules, these are never turned into multiple-choice problems.

subtypes.acceleration: Compute the acceleration over a (possibly auto-picked) interval.
subtypes.displacement: Compute the displacement (= signed area under v-t) over an interval. Defaults to the whole hand-drawn range if params.interval is omitted.
subtypes.direction: Identify the interval(s) where velocity is negative (motion in the opposite direction).
subtypes.describe: Produce a model-answer description of the motion over a (possibly auto-picked) interval.

optionalFields: params.interval={t0,t1} — explicit interval; otherwise picked deterministically via a seed derived from the graph

graphChoice.name: Graph multiple-choice (API-only — not exposed in the browser UI)
graphChoice.description: Per CONTEXT.md, this type exists only in the API: students should hand-draw graphs in the UI, so multiple-choice graph problems are intentionally absent from the browser UI. 'Which graph correctly corresponds to the shown v-t/x-t graph?' — student picks one of N graphs.
graphChoice.distractors: IMPORTANT: this tool does not generate distractor graphs itself — the caller (an AI agent) must supply each distractor as a full MotionGraph spec (same shape as 'source'). The tool only ensures consistency, layout and output of the supplied graphs.
graphChoice.choiceOrdering: KinematicsProblemGenerator.generateGraphChoice ALREADY shuffles internally and returns the final display order with correctIndex + seed — the API does not re-shuffle.

choices.enabled: boolean — required (true) for type='graphChoice', forbidden for other types
choices.distractors: Array of full MotionGraph specs (same shape as 'source'), length must equal count - 1
choices.shuffle: default true. Seeded deterministically by hash of (source + sourceKind + askFor + distractors + x0) — same input always produces the same shuffle.
choices.response: files.choices is an array in DISPLAY order (already final — the generator shuffles internally). Each entry has {path|dataUrl, isCorrect, label}. response.correctIndex is the 0-based index of the correct choice in display order.

output.default: Files written to ./api_output/{sessionId}/. Response includes absolute paths.
output.outputDir: Override the directory (must be an absolute path).
output.inline: If true, response contains base64 dataUrls instead of file paths. No files are written.
output.manifest.json: Saved alongside files; contains the full request + response (for reproducibility).

graphConversionVtStep._note: vt-step sources are always velocity-time (sourceKind defaults to 'vt-step'); a-t will show undefined/impulsive instants at the jump boundaries.

graphChoiceShapeOnly._note: displayPreset 'shape-only' hides axis labels/units/ticks so students must pick the correct x-t graph by shape alone. 'display' can override single keys, e.g. "display": { "showGrid": true }.
